## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Swissquote was founded in **1996** in **Gland, Switzerland** — making it one of the oldest online trading platforms in Europe. The company started as an online bank and broker, providing Swiss-quality financial services to retail and institutional clients globally. Swissquote is unique among forex brokers as it operates as both a **licensed bank** (Swissquote Bank AG) and a **regulated broker** (Swissquote Ltd UK, Swissquote Ltd Cyprus, etc.).

In 2000, Swissquote was **listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange** (ticker: SQN), becoming one of the few publicly traded forex brokers globally. This public listing provides an additional level of transparency, as the company is required to publish quarterly financial reports, annual reports, and material corporate announcements.

The company's name reflects its core value proposition: "Swiss" quality and reliability combined with "quote" — access to financial markets and pricing. Swissquote has consistently positioned itself as a premium broker, emphasizing security, regulation, and Swiss banking heritage.

## 1. Regulatory Deep Dive

### Overview

Swissquote is a fully licensed Swiss bank and one of Europe's leading online banking and trading providers. Founded in 1996, it operates under the supervision of the Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority (FINMA), which is considered one of the strictest financial regulators globally. Swissquote is publicly listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange (SQN) and provides both traditional banking services (savings accounts, mortgages, credit cards) and online trading (forex, CFDs, stocks, ETFs, options, futures, cryptocurrencies). This dual banking + trading model makes Swissquote unique among brokers.

### License Portfolio

#### License #1: Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority (FINMA) — Switzerland

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Regulator** | Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority (FINMA) |
| **License Number** | Banking Licence |
| **Verification Link** | [FINMA Register of Banks](https://www.finma.ch/en/banks/banks/) |
| **Entity Name** | Swissquote Bank Ltd |
| **Status** | ✅ Licensed & Supervised (Active) |
| **Jurisdiction** | Switzerland |
| **License Type** | Full Banking License — Securities Dealer, Asset Manager |
| **Date Obtained** | 2000 (banking license) |

**What This Means For You:**

- **Leverage Limits:** Retail clients: 30:1 for major forex, 20:1 for non-major forex, 10:1 for commodities, 5:1 for shares, 2:1 for crypto. Professional clients can access higher leverage.
- **Deposit Protection:** esisuisse (Swiss deposit insurance) — up to CHF 100,000 (approximately USD 112,000) per depositor per bank
- **Negative Balance Protection:** ✅ Yes — guaranteed for retail clients
- **Complaint Process:** Internal complaints → Swiss Banking Ombudsman → FINMA
- **Segregation of Funds:** ✅ Yes — client assets held in separate accounts under Swiss banking law

**Countries Served Under This License:**
- ✅ Switzerland, EU/EEA (via various branch registrations)
- ❌ USA (limited — Swissquote US is a separate entity), Japan
